untranslatable_reason: This signal requires option-specific indicators (implied volatility, delta, leverage ratio, moneyness, strike prices) that are not available in the provided indicator set. The rule compares implied volatility spreads between options and leverage ratios between comparable strikes—concepts that require options pricing data and Greeks (delta, vega, theta) not present in standard OHLCV technical analysis indicators.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_34_0 is an options relative value selection rule from 'The Volatility Smile' or similar derivatives textbook. It requires: (1) implied volatility comparison across strikes, (2) delta/leverage calculations, (3) moneyness classification. These are derivatives analytics rather than spot price technical indicators. To implement this signal, a backtest engine would need to integrate an options pricing library (Black-Scholes, binomial, etc.) and compute Greeks in real-time. The signal cannot be expressed using only price, volume, and technical indicator data.
